title: Rahul, Kharge write to PM; seek probe into theft of donations at Ayodhya Ram temple
content: Congress leaders demand that the findings and the Trust’s accounts should be made public so that every devotee knows how their offerings have been utilised

title: Opposition walks out of all-party meeting over presence of rebel Trinamool faction
content: The breakaway TMC faction claims to have joined the Nationalist Citizens Party of India; the government is expected to outline its legislative agenda during the meeting

title: Man found murdered in Kozhikode
content: Involvement of drug addicts suspected based on the statements of some of the local residents in the area

title: Final rites of Chennamma Deve Gowda at foothills of Ranganatha Swamy Betta at Mavinakere
content: The mortal remains of Chennamma will be brought to Holenarasipur by Sunday (July 19, 2026) evening. The public will be allowed to pay their last respects to her at the residence of former Minister H. D. Revanna in Holenarasipur

title: Police action forces end to tribal protest against MP’s Ken-Betwa project after 15 days
content: The police dismantled the protest site, on the banks of Barana river near Kupi village, and symbolic pyres used by the demonstrators

title: BJP claiming ‘fake credit’ for raising land acquisition compensation for farmers; law made by UPA govt, says Congress
content: The Madhya Pradesh Cabinet had approved an increase in the multiplication factor applicable to land acquisition in rural areas from 1.0 to 2.0. to provide farmers with compensation up to four times the market price

title: Sonam Wangchuk calls July 20 Parliament march India's 'second freedom movement'
content: The note, shared on X through Mr. Wangchuk's account, read: "20th July. India's second freedom movement. Freedom from injustice (like paper leaks). Freedom from fear [my illegal detention]."

title: The fine art of creating blood donors at home; and resources for those in Chennai seeking to donate blood
content: A leitmotif among blood donation organisations is the increasing difficulty in finding people with “generous veins”; and the solution could strangely be found in the living rooms of households, as illustrated by these two accounts from Chennai of parents inspiring their children to join the cause

title: Jammu and Kashmir rains LIVE: Flash floods, landslips wreak havoc in Poonch-Rajouri; eight dead, six missing
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah spoke to Jammu and Kashmir L-G Manoj Sinha and CM Omar Abdullah to take stock of the flood situation in Rajouri district.

title: J&K: Flash floods, landslides wreak havoc in Poonch-Rajouri; eight dead, six missing
content: Dozens of vehicles were swept away or submerged after overflowing rivers breached their banks, causing widespread disruption, the officials said

title: CM Vijay urged to rename Udhagamandalam
content: The popular name of Ootacamund, or Ooty in short, was inexplicably Sanskritised as Udhagamandalam in 1972

title: With her dance moves, this conservancy worker from Chennai has become the new face of social media algorithm
content: N. Senbagavalli is many things rolled into one: a battery-operated vehicle operator with Urbaser Sumeet in the morning,  an autodriver in the evening, and a successful digital creator on Instagram in between

title: 72nd National Film Awards: Telugu cinema shines with 9 awards; ‘Kalki 2898 AD,’ ‘Pushpa 2’ win big
content: Kalki 2898 AD, Committee Kurrollu, 35-Chinna Katha Kaadu and Pushpa 2: The Rule won two awards each, while Lucky Baskhar bagged one

title: J&K: Amarnath, Vaishno Devi, Shiv Khori pilgrimages suspended amid rain forecast
content: “In view of inclement weather forecast issued by IMD, Shri Amarnathji Yatra shall remain temporarily suspended from both the Pahalgam and Baltal routes with effect from 19.07.2026 as a precautionary measure to ensure the safety and well-being of the pilgrims,” says Kashmir Divisional Commissioner

title: Sonam Wangchuk’s wife Gitanjali Angmo moves Delhi HC to shift him from Safdarjung hospital
content: Gitanjali J. Angmo has sought an urgent hearing; she said that she lost her faith in Safdarjung Government Hospital in Delhi

title: Where football is freedom: A Kolkata mixed-gender team is rewriting rules from margins
content: As the FIFA World Cup commands global attention, children from Kolkata’s Kalighat red-light area are fighting a different battle for the game—one for playgrounds, dignity and the right to dream

title: ‘Wangchuk not alone’: At least 21 continue indefinite hunger strike
content: Three AISA activists enter 21st day of hunger strike; protesters have been advised by doctors to rest as much as possible, conserve energy and speak less

title: Sonam Wangchuk forcibly hospitalised; CJP founder begins fast, vows to march to Parliament on July 20
content: Police cite Delhi High Court order after moving Mr. Wangchuk to Safdarjung Hospital, where he refused medical interventions; Opposition leaders say government is repressing peaceful dissent

title: News in Frames: The weight of a nation’s food
content: From dawn until dusk, they lift, carry, stack and unload sacks of grain and agricultural commodities, keeping the flow of food moving from farms to markets

title: NMC will no longer issue eligibility certificates to doctors seeking teaching posts in routine cases
content: More complicated cases must take the institutional route, with a fee of ₹25,000 plus tax; move aims to promote institutional accountability in faculty appointments and promotions, reduce admin burden on Board

title: What makes Skyroot’s Vikram-1 launch unique? | Explained
content: Skyroot’s Vikram-1 has made India only the third country where a private company has independently placed payloads in orbit. Here’s what makes the mission significant

title: How will U.S. visa policy changes affect students, media professionals, and exchange visitors | Explained
content: The U.S. Department of Homeland Security cites national security risks from Indian and Chinese students and introduces changes in F, J and I categories of visas. PhD scholars, journalists, and exchange visitors will be impacted

title: Delimitation not just an INDIA bloc issue, concerns our Constitutional framework’ says Congress’ K.C. Venugopal
content: “Delimitation is not a Congress issue or an INDIA bloc issue alone; it is a national issue because it concerns India’s democratic and constitutional framework,” Mr. Venugopal said

title: CBSE declares class 10 second board exam results
content: The results for the Class 10 Second Board Exams can be accessed by students through their DigiLocker Results Portal on results.digilocker.gov.in

title: As Vikram-1 reaches orbit, Skyroot faces a steep climb to business success
content: A single launch — crucial though it is — will not catapult Skyroot to success. The global small-satellite launch market is evolving and there are still considerable challenges, including domestic demand and market uncertainties

title: Sonam Wangchuk hunger strike updates: Wangchuk continues his fast in hospital
content: An independent expert from AIIMS has concurred with the treating team that Sonam Wangchuk needs "immediate medical intervention", hospital said in its latest bulletin

title: Faith and its fallout
content: In Ayodhya, weeks after allegations of money being siphoned off from donations at the Ram temple surfaced, pilgrims and residents say it has triggered a crisis of trust. Devotees say their faith in Lord Ram and the temple remains intact, but many have grown cautious about donating. Locals report a dip in visitors, though they attribute part of that to seasonal factors as well. As an SIT probe seeks accountability, Ishita Mishra reports from the temple town, where faith, transparency, and the city’s religious tourism hopes hang in the balance

title: Quicksand in Chennai’s Pallikaranai marshland
content: The first expansion of the city of Chennai in modern times happened along its southern stretch. As the government in its wisdom decided to locate the IT corridor in south Chennai, breathless development happened over a couple of decades, right around a rich natural reserve, the Pallikaranai marshland. Today, two decades later, there’s a conflict that pits development against preservation. This report attempts to pull together the several threads that have brought this particular tapestry to the fore today

title: ‘Not just Statehood’: PDP chief lists conditions for joining NC’s Delhi protest
content: In letter to Farooq Abdullah, Mufti says party may skip agitation unless restoration of Article 370, release of political prisoners and lifting of the ban on the Jamaat-e-Islami are included in agenda

title: New tool to help cities quantify greenhouse gas emissions from food waste
content: The Excel-based tool developed by WRI aims to help cities, businesses, and resident associations reduce emissions; it estimates emissions from transportation, treatment, and disposal of waste

title: T.N. government to celebrate State’s history on both July 18 and November 1, says Vijay
content: When the AIADMK was in power, the then Chief Minister Edappadi K. Palaniswami announced that November 1 would be celebrated as Tamil Nadu Day. However, after the DMK came to power in 2021, it changed the observance to July 18

title: Mother, son complete their IIT-M programmes together
content: While Aditya Kapadia completed his BS degree programme in Data Science and Applications, his mother completed a Diploma in Data Science

title: Mines and Geology Department recommends referring Ballari mining representations to Justice Dhulia committee
content: Mines and Geology Department says it is not the competent authority to decide the issues raised in the representation

title: NC expels Ladakh leader Akhoon as he refuses to step down as CEC of Kargil hill council
content: The action follows Dr. Mohammad Jaffer Akhoon’s refusal to step down as Chief Executive Councillor of the Ladakh Autonomous Hill Development Council, failing to honour the NC-Congress 2023 pre-poll agreement for power sharing

title: PM to inaugurate Bhogapuram International Airport on August 1
content: The green field airport is poised to reshape the local economic landscape of the region, says Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu

title: Man held for smuggling ganja in government bus at Katpadi
content: After driving through Chittoor, the bus halted at the check post near Katpadi when a team of police personnel checked the vehicle and luggage of commuters

title: Annual sports day held in Walajah
content: As part of celebrations, track and field events, including relay race, long jump, high jump, javelin throw, discus throw, kabaddi, football, and volleyball were conducted for students

title: Parliament session, CJP's rally plan key reasons for Wangchuk's removal from protest site
content: Security agencies were also apprehensive of law and order situation in the national capital in view of CJP’s proposed rally

title: Minister inaugurates synthetic hockey turf, Kodai Vizha in Tiruvannamalai
content: The new hockey turf, which will be maintained by SDAT has a spectators gallery with a seating capacity of over 480 persons to watch hockey and athletics events

title: Stone laid for road development works worth ₹3.02 crore in Vijayawada
content: The projects, funded under the 15th Finance Commission’s Air Quality Improvement Programme, are aimed at improving urban infrastructure while reducing dust pollution

title: Uddhav Thackeray calls for ‘BJP-free’ Ram movement
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) chief criticises the BJP-led Union government over alleged financial misappropriation at the Ayodhya Ram temple and the police action on protesters at Jantar Mantar

title: Sleepless nights, depressing times, and then a Grandmaster: Making of Rathanvel VS
content: After four-and-a-half years of heartbreak, sleepless nights and repeated near-misses, Rathanvel VS finally crossed the 2500 Elo mark to become India's 99th Grandmaster. From discovering chess through a free board to overcoming injuries, financial setbacks and crushing disappointments, the 2001-born Coimbatore player's perseverance, family support and belief transformed a long-awaited dream into reality.

title: US judge to Meta's laid off employees: Cannot stop the company from job cuts
content: A US judge has allowed Meta Platforms to proceed with planned layoffs of 26 employees. These employees had sued the company alleging AI-driven discrimination in job cuts. The judge declined an emergency request to halt the layoffs while claims proceed. He stated the court may reconsider if new evidence emerges about AI usage. Meta denies wrongdoing and states human decisions guided the workforce reduction.

title: Messi aims to accomplish in New York what Maradona couldn't 36 years ago
content: It is also the first time since Diego Maradona inspired Argentina to back-to-back World Cup finals in 1986 and 1990 that the country has reached consecutive title matches. Argentina won the World Cup in 1986 before finishing runners-up in 1990. Their seven final appearances have come in 1930, 1978, 1986, 1990, 2014, 2022 and 2026.

title: Punjab cop, who targeted NRIs in US, held in Rs 3.8-cr extortion bid linked to AAP worker's murder
content: Punjab Police arrested inspector Gurinderjit Singh Nagra, former Tanda SHO.  He faces charges of extortion and corruption linked to a US family.  The US justice department seeks his extradition for conspiracy to commit extortion.  Nagra allegedly extorted Rs 16 lakh from the US-based family.  This case is connected to the murder of an AAP worker.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: China building world's largest naval replenishment ship to boost carrier operations
content: China is developing a new and larger naval replenishment vessel at Guangzhou Shipyard. This advanced vessel is expected to exceed the size of current Type 901 support ships, bolstering the People's Liberation Army Navy's ability to operate effectively at long ranges. Its impressive dimensions may even rival those of the US Navy's replenishment fleet, reflecting China's ambitions for extended carrier operations beyond its territorial waters.

title: Mount Olympus, mythological home of Greek gods, may get UNESCO status
content: Mount Olympus, a site of myth and nature, seeks UNESCO World Heritage recognition.  This ancient Greek mountain is home to gods and diverse ecosystems.  Locals hope for global acknowledgment to ensure its continued protection.  The nomination process involves careful review and potential further requirements.  Recognition would bring responsibilities for preserving its unique cultural and natural values.

title: Musk responds to user on Tesla Self-Driving getting stuck in slow traffic
content: Tesla's Full Self-Driving system will soon learn individual driver preferences.  The software will adapt its behavior based on manual driver interventions.  This change follows a user complaint about exiting faster lanes.  Elon Musk stated the vehicle will remember specific driver actions.  Future updates will offer a more personalized self-driving experience for owners.

title: Before kick-off, Argentina vs Spain final has already entered World Cup history
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 final features Argentina and Spain, the top two ranked teams.  Argentina seeks consecutive titles, a feat not achieved since Brazil in 1962.  Spain aims for their second World Cup, boasting an impressive thirty-seven-game unbeaten streak.  This match marks Lionel Messi's first encounter with Spain since 2010.  Spain's defense has been exceptional, conceding only one goal throughout the tournament.

title: 'Your silence is unacceptable': Rahul, Kharge write to PM Modi on Ram Mandir donation theft
content: Demanding a probe into financial affairs of Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, both the leaders said that the "findings and the Trust's accounts should be made public so that every devotee knows how their offerings have been utilised". ​Ahead of the Monoon Session of the Parliament, the leader of the opposition in the Lok Sabha, Rahul Gandhi, and the Rajya Sabha, Mallikarjun Kharge, urged PM to "immediately order an independent and comprehensive investigation.

title: Maple Leafs' Gavin McKenna trains with Sharks star Macklin Celebrini
content: Toronto Maple Leafs’ prospect Gavin McKenna was recently spotted in a competitive offseason skate in Vancouver with some of hockey's brightest young talent. The training session gave fans a glimpse of the next generation of the NHL with San Jose Sharks forward Macklin Celebrini, Chicago Blackhawks prospect Michael Misa and San Jose Sharks defenseman Sam Dickinson.

title: How much prize money does the FIFA World Cup 2026 third-place winner get? Full payout explained
content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will award twenty-nine million dollars for third place. The fourth-place team will receive twenty-seven million dollars after the playoff. This prize money is separate from preparation payments given to nations. The champions will earn fifty million dollars and runners-up thirty-three million dollars. FIFA confirmed prize money distribution for all tournament stages.

title: Former NHL star Mike Commodore warns Oilers parents at development camp
content: Ex-NHL defenseman Mike Commodore has escalated his years-long war on the Edmonton Oilers by issuing a warning to parents attending the club’s development camp. The social media post went viral across the NHL, reigniting discussion of Commodore’s past with former coach Mike Babcock, and drawing mixed reactions from hockey fans and analysts.

title: PV Sindhu scripts history, stuns Yamaguchi to become first Indian to ...
content: The 31-year-old became the first Indian to win the Japan Open after beating the three-time world champion 21-17, 21-17. The victory ended her title drought of more than two years and marked her biggest title since winning the World Championships in 2019.

title: 'Ramayana': Ranbir Kapoor touches Sunny Deol and Kumar Vishwas' feet at the Pratham Sankalp event
content: At 'Ramayana's Pratham Sankalp event, Ranbir Kapoor touched Sunny Deol and Kumar Vishwas's feet, winning hearts online. Ranbir, Sai Pallavi and Sunny shared heartfelt moments with media. Starring Ranbir, Sai Pallavi, Yash and Sunny, the Rs 4,000 crore epic releases in two parts—Diwali 2026 and Diwali 2027.

title: Call records, chats, deleted files: Chilling details in Lucknow scientist's death
content: Police are examining Rahul Singh's smartphone for digital evidence related to his death.  An FIR was registered against four individuals for abetment to suicide charges.  Rahul's brother alleged sustained harassment and financial disputes led to his extreme step.  Investigators are reviewing communications and financial transactions for crucial details.  The forensic report will determine the exact cause of the scientist's death.

title: Contaminated needle? Kanpur woman tests HIV+ weeks after tattoo in Delhi
content: A 32-year-old woman from Kanpur contracted HIV after a severe infection developed post-tattoo. Doctors suspect a contaminated needle at a Delhi parlour caused the transmission. She has begun free Anti-Retroviral Therapy at a government hospital for management. Unhygienic tattoo practices can expose individuals to blood-borne infections like HIV. Tattoo inks may also trigger various skin disorders and reactions.

title: 13 matches and counting! Messi's Argentina become the first team ever to ...
content: Argentina head into the FIFA World Cup 2026 final against Spain after becoming the first team to score at least two goals in 13 consecutive World Cup matches. The defending champions have also scored in 16 straight World Cup games. Lionel Scaloni's side now aims to extend both streaks as they chase back-to-back titles against Spain.

title: 'Everything is lost': Overnight rain brings J&K's Rajouri to a standstill
content: More than 50 families living near Abdullah Bridge were forced to leave their homes after floodwaters flooded the settlement. Another locality near Tariq Bridge was also affected, prompting police to respond to distress calls and help residents move to safer places away from the riverbanks. Rescue and relief teams, assisted by the district administration, were deployed to evacuate affected families and assess the damage.

title: 'India's 2nd freedom movement': Wangchuk's message from hospital ahead of CJP's July 20 march
content: Activist Sonam Wangchuk, shifted to Safdarjung Hospital on the 21st day of his hunger strike, called for support for the July 20 Parliament march, describing his hospital stay as “illegal detention”. Police said the move followed medical advice and court directions, while protest organisers vowed to continue their agitation.

title: Decision to privatise space sector proved right: PM Modi to Skyroot after Vikram-1 launch
content: India achieved a historic milestone with the successful launch of its first private orbital rocket. Prime Minister Narendra Modi lauded the achievement, emphasizing faith in the nation's youth. This success positions India as the third country with indigenous private orbital launch capabilities. Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket carried a special message for the universe. The event signifies a new era for India's growing private space sector.

title: Mbappe breaks Messi's all-time WC goal record; Saka hat-trick fails France in 10-goal thriller
content: England emerged victorious in an exhilarating 6-4 clash against France during the third-place playoff. Kylian Mbappé marked his place in history as the World Cup's leading goal scorer. Bukayo Saka showcased his talent by netting a remarkable hat-trick, while Jude Bellingham secured England's bronze medal with a final flourish. France's aspirations to equalize fell short amid the high-scoring excitement of the match.

title: Who is Pawan Kumar Chandana? The IIT graduate behind Vikram-1
content: Pawan Kumar Chandana left ISRO to co-found Skyroot Aerospace with a bold vision.  The company's Vikram-1 rocket successfully reached orbit, marking a significant private space achievement.  This launch proved private Indian companies can build world-class rockets independently.  Skyroot now employs over a thousand people and has attracted substantial investment.  This success signals India's growing ambition in the global commercial space industry.

title: Former live-in partner slits woman's throat in Nashik, found hanging hours later
content: A 21-year-old woman was fatally stabbed by her estranged live-in partner in Nashik. The accused then died by suicide hours after the violent attack occurred. The victim had recently ended their relationship and moved to Nashik for work. Police are registering a case based on a complaint from the victim's fiancé. The incident highlights a tragic end to a troubled relationship.

title: Beyond tiki-taka: Tactical evolution that has Spain one win from FIFA World Cup glory
content: Spain have quietly put together one of the most complete tactical campaigns of the World Cup, reaching the final with a new identity that goes beyond the tiki-taka era. Under Luis de la Fuente, La Roja have blended control with verticality, aggression and defensive discipline, shutting down elite attacks while playing with purpose. Now, only Lionel Messi's Argentina stand between Spain and a second World Cup title.

title: MLC: LA Knight Riders clinch maiden title with one-run win over Freedom
content: Needing 14 runs off the final over, Washington Freedom looked favourites after Obus Pienaar smashed the first ball for six. But Shadley van Schalkwyk produced three wickets in the last five deliveries, including a run-out off the final ball, to spark wild celebrations as the LA Knight Riders became MLC's newest champions.

title: 64-year-old record broken! England vs France rewrite FIFA World Cup history
content: England secured a 6-4 victory over France in a thrilling World Cup third-place playoff. Bukayo Saka scored a hat-trick, inspiring England's dominant performance throughout the match. Kylian Mbappe also netted twice, surpassing Lionel Messi's World Cup scoring record. This high-scoring contest marked the end of Didier Deschamps' tenure as France's head coach. The memorable match captivated a sell-out crowd at Hard Rock Stadium.

title: US military death toll in Iran war rises to 16 after two troops killed in Jordan
content: Two additional US service members died defending against Iranian attacks in Jordan.  This brings the total American military deaths in the conflict to sixteen.  The ongoing conflict is largely fought through drones and missile strikes.  Iranian authorities reported at least fifty people killed and over five hundred injured.  Civilians and foreign workers across the region have also suffered casualties.

title: ‘The Odyssey’ box office collection day 2: Nolan's epic mints Rs 22 cr in India
content: Christopher Nolan's film 'The Odyssey' is performing strongly at the Indian box office.  The movie collected a net total of twenty-two crore rupees on its second day.  Its gross collection reached forty-seven crore rupees, with net earnings at thirty-nine crore.  The English version led the collection, earning seventeen crore rupees on Saturday.  'The Odyssey' is expected to surpass fifty crore rupees by its third day.

title: Child rape-murder case: Brutality alone no ground for death, says MP HC
content: A division bench of the Madhya Pradesh High Court's Gwalior bench commuted the death sentence of a man convicted of raping and murdering a nine-year-old girl to life imprisonment for the remainder of his natural life. While upholding his conviction, the court ruled the case was heinous but did not meet the “rarest of rare” standard.

title: Jude Bellingham breaks all-time England goalscoring record
content: In an impressive World Cup run, Jude Bellingham scored seven goals, featuring a brilliant solo strike that led England to a third-place win. His record-breaking achievement outshined past legends in the sport and silenced doubts about his place in the lineup, paving the way for future generations of English players to aspire to his legacy.

title: Despite 35-minute delay, Vikram-1 keeps date with Indian space history with Mission Aagaman success
content: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket successfully launched into low Earth orbit on Saturday.  This historic mission placed six payloads into their targeted orbit successfully.  India now becomes the third nation with private orbital launch capability.  The Hyderabad-based startup achieved this significant milestone on its first attempt.  This launch signifies a new era for private Indian space industry operations.

title: 'He's not the best': Ex-India coach makes bold Messi claim before World Cup final
content: Stephen Constantine believes Lionel Messi is not football's greatest player ever. He ranks Pelé, Maradona, and Ronaldinho above the Argentine star. Constantine suggests Argentina's tactics help Messi conserve energy during matches. He predicts Spain will defeat Argentina in the World Cup final. The former India coach highlights Spain's collective work ethic as key.

title: "Illegal Detention": Sonam Wangchuk's Wife Seeks Urgent High Court Hearing
content: Sonam Wangchuk's wife has accused Safdarjung hospital of a lack of transparency in his treatment and sought his discharge.

title: 1 Dead, Vehicles Swept Away As Flash Floods Hit J&K's Rajouri
content: Flash floods triggered by heavy rains flood Jammu's Rajouri, damaging infrastructure and forcing evacuations.

title: Delayed Orders, Missing Gear: What Hindered Venezuela's Quake Response
content: Civilians spearheaded much of the response, especially during the first two days, bringing aid like food and using rudimentary tools to pull both the living and the dead from the rubble.

title: 'School Fees Are Just The Beginning': The Hidden Cost Of Educating Children
content: Tuition classes, digital devices, transport, uniforms, extracurricular activities, and competitive exam coaching have made education a costly affair.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: US Says Latest Strikes Targeted Iran Guards Behind Jordan Attack
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: Iran claimed it destroyed "several US refuelling aircraft and fighter jets" and caused "serious damage to many more".

title: Who Is Gitanjali Angmo, Sonam Wangchuk's Biggest Pillar Of Support
content: Gitanjali Angmo, wife of Sonam Wangchuk, is best known as the co-founder of the Himalayan Institute of Alternatives, Ladakh.

title: "He's War Criminal": Zohran Mamdani Wants To Arrest Netanyahu During New York Trip
content: Zohran Mamdani said he is not sure if he has the power to order the arrest of a foreign leader and that he is consulting the city's legal advisers regarding the issue.

title: US Strikes To "Punish" Iran, Tehran Vows To Teach "Unforgettable Lessons"
content: The US launched the eighth consecutive night of strikes hours after announcing that two American soldiers were killed in Iran's attack on Jordan.

title: Tree Felling For Rishikesh-Bhaniyawala Project Halted Amid Public Outrage
content: Uttarakhand Chief Minister suspends tree felling for Rishikesh road project until consensus is reached.

title: Amarnath, Vaishno Devi Yatras To Be Temporarily Suspended From July 19 Amid Weather Forecast
content: Amarnath and Vaishno Devi Yatras suspended from July 19 due to inclement weather forecast in Jammu and Kashmir.

title: 'I'll Quit Within An Hour If You...': Abhishek Banerjee's Dare To Rebels
content: Abhishek Banerjee was responding to a question on the ever-increasing defections from the TMC in the aftermath of its assembly poll debacle, leaving Mamata Banerjee hanging by a thread as far as her control over the party.

title: Andrew Tate, His Brother Tristan Arrested In US Over Rape, Sex Trafficking Charges In UK
content: British prosecutors announced Saturday that they were seeking the Tate brothers' extradition on charges alleging they raped and trafficked women between 2010 and 2017.

title: Skyroot's Vikram-1 Launch Highlights: "Grand Success," Says Skyroot As Vikram-1 Successfully Releases Payload In Space
content: Skyroot Vikram-1 Launch, Mission Aagaman Highlights: The launch is seen as a major milestone for India's rapidly expanding private space sector.

title: Sonam Wangchuk's Fast, Ram Mandir Row To Dominate All-Party Meet Today
content: Additionally, the government has announced five new bills that will be introduced in the Lok Sabha or the Rajya Sabha during the upcoming Monsoon Session.

title: BJP Starts Outreach Campaign In Muslim-Dominated Assembly Seat Of Manipur
content: Manipur Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh on Saturday said that his main aim is to try to unite the people of Manipur after the three-year-long ethnic violence.

title: 2 Robberies, 1 Chilling Pattern: Bihar Village Fears "Hair-Chopping" Gang
content: The incidents, which took place eight days apart, have fuelled talk of a so-called "Baal Katarva" or hair-chopping gang operating in the area.

title: AIIMS expert backs urgent care for Wangchuk, activist denies

title: Aleph and the endgame: Spain stand between Messi and football immortality
content: the eponymous character is a mystical point in space that contains all other points in the universe, allowing whoever looks into it to see every place on Earth simultaneously without distortion . this Sunday, Lionel Messi — the Aleph in Argentina’s football atmosphere — will seek the infinite at the very place in New Jersey where, lonely and defeated a decade ago, he had decided to quit the game .
